plan.visual_reading: The arc opens on the mockers' faces at the foot of the cross — shot-out lips, shaking heads — then steps back a thousand years to David recording those very gestures and words on his scroll. The proof movement intercuts the two scenes: the Hebrew taunt and the cross-side crowd doing exactly what was written. The conviction turns the camera up to Christ, sovereign and unhurried, the power He held back rendered as half-dissolved restrained legions. The landing rests on His face looking down in love and the full cross 'with you in view' — the close lands on the crucified Christ, the gospel-pivot.
plan.red_team_notes: Jaded Viewer risks: a generic 'angry crowd' could read as any mob — so the mockers are anchored to the SPECIFIC Psalm 22:7 actions (shot-out lip, shaken head, pointed finger), not vague rage. Two scroll scenes (2, 12) risk overlap — kept distinct (David writing vs the thousand-years diptych). The 'restrained legions' scene (9) risks fantasy-glow angels — anchored as half-dissolved shadow-memory, not a glowing host. The hero must be the cross, not the most emotional face — so #11 (full crucified Christ) is hero, with #10 (face in love) as the emotional climax feeding it.

scenes.title: The Shaking Heads
scenes.purpose: Hook on the mockers' gesture at the cross
scenes.rationale: the specific Psalm 22:7 actions, not a generic angry mob
scenes.visible_elements: two or three jeering faces with shot-out lips and heads tilted in scorn, the dark upright of a cross edge behind
scenes.subject_block: a tight view of jeering faces at the foot of a rough wooden cross, lips curled and shot out in scorn, heads tilted and shaken mid-mockery, eyes narrowed upward, weathered first-century robes in dust and ochre, the dark grain of the cross upright just visible at the edge, deep shadow swallowing the crowd beyond
macro_elements: the dark grain of the cross upright

scenes.title: David Records the Taunt
scenes.purpose: The taunt written a thousand years early
scenes.rationale: David inscribing the mockers' own words (Ps 22:7-8)
scenes.visible_elements: aged David at a low table, a Hebrew scroll, an oil lamp, faint background vignettes of mockery
scenes.subject_block: an aged King David seated at a low cedar table, a feather pen at rest above a half-unrolled parchment dense with Hebrew letters, a small oil lamp throwing warm light across his lined brow, subtle background vignettes fading into shadow suggesting shaken heads, shot-out lips, a jabbing pointed finger, and a far wooden cross
macro_elements: a line of wet Hebrew letters

scenes.title: Let Him Deliver Him
scenes.rationale: a 2s insert on the prophesied taunt
scenes.visible_elements: extreme close on a Hebrew scroll line, a fingertip beneath it
scenes.subject_block: an extreme close view of an open parchment, a single weathered fingertip at rest beneath one illuminated line of ancient Hebrew script, the ink old and cracked, the parchment fibrous and warmly lamplit, deep shadow at the edges

scenes.title: The Crowd at the Cross
scenes.rationale: passers-by wagging + leaders jeering, Christ above
scenes.visible_elements: a crowd at the foot of the cross, wagging heads and jabbing fingers, Christ on the cross above
scenes.subject_block: a wide view at the foot of a tall wooden cross, a knot of passers-by with heads wagged in scorn and robed religious leaders with fingers jabbed upward, the crucified Christ robed at the waist just above them against a darkened sky, subtle background vignettes fading into shadow suggesting wagging heads, a sneering elder, jabbing fingers, and the dark crossbeam
scenes.mood_block: atmosphere of prophecy fulfilled amid scorn

scenes.title: The Religious Leaders Jeer
scenes.rationale: Matt 27:41-43 chief priests, scribes and elders
scenes.visible_elements: robed religious leaders pointing up and sneering
scenes.subject_block: two robed religious leaders in rich layered robes and prayer fringes, faces lifted in smug contempt, one with a finger extended upward toward the cross, the other's lip curled, dim onlookers behind dissolving into shadow

scenes.title: They Shoot Out the Lip
scenes.rationale: the overlooked 'shoot out the lip' detail
scenes.visible_elements: a single mocking face, lip thrust out
scenes.subject_block: a single mocking face filling the view, the lower lip thrust out and curled in derision, teeth just bared, eyes lifted in cold scorn, weathered skin in raking light, the rest sunk in shadow

scenes.title: The Jabbing Hands
scenes.rationale: hands as the gesture of accusation
scenes.visible_elements: several hands with fingers jabbed upward
scenes.subject_block: several weathered hands raised at the foot of the cross, fingers jabbed and pointed upward in accusation, sleeves of dusty robes falling back from the wrists, the pale sky beyond, deep shadow below

scenes.title: He Had Every Power
scenes.rationale: Christ unhurried, full of power, looking down
scenes.visible_elements: Christ on the cross from below, calm and sovereign
scenes.subject_block: the crucified Christ seen from a low angle against a darkened sky, robed at the waist, head unbowed and gaze turned downward with unhurried calm, arms outstretched along the beam, a faint sorrowful strength in his face, the small mockers far below in shadow

scenes.title: Twelve Legions Restrained
scenes.rationale: Matt 26:53 — more than twelve legions of angels, restrained
scenes.visible_elements: Christ on the cross, dim restrained ranks half-dissolved in shadow
scenes.subject_block: the crucified Christ robed at the waist, head lowered in stillness, subtle background vignettes fading into shadow suggesting dim ranks of restrained angelic figures half-dissolved in the dark sky, a sheathed sword, a crown of thorns, and a single nailed hand held open
scenes.mood_block: atmosphere of withheld omnipotence and chosen love

scenes.title: Looking Down in Love
scenes.rationale: the emotional climax feeding the gospel close
scenes.visible_elements: Christ's face, eyes lowered in love
scenes.subject_block: a close reverent view of the crucified Christ's face, head inclined, eyes lowered with tender willing love rather than pain, a thorn-marked brow, faint warm light on one cheek against deep shadow, the grain of the cross at his shoulder

scenes.title: He Chose to Stay
scenes.rationale: the iconic full crucified Christ, with you in view
scenes.visible_elements: the full crucified Christ on the cross, calm, against a soft break of light
scenes.subject_block: the crucified Christ on a rough wooden cross from a respectful middle distance, arms outstretched along the beam, body robed at the waist, head gently inclined, a soft break of pale light widening behind the dark hill, the mockers and the heaped garments small and shadowed below

scenes.title: A Thousand Years Apart
scenes.rationale: David's scroll and the cross across a thousand years
scenes.visible_elements: David at his scroll on the left, the distant cross on the right
scenes.subject_block: a wide two-sided composition split by a corridor of deep shadow; at the left, aged David seated at his oil lamp with a feather pen at rest on a wet-inked scroll; at the right, across the divide, the crucified Christ on a distant cross beneath a stormy sky, subtle background vignettes fading into shadow suggesting the wet Hebrew taunt, a shaken head, jabbing fingers, and the far cross
scenes.mood_block: atmosphere of quiet wonder across a thousand years

scenes.title: The Mockers and the Silent King
scenes.purpose: the contrast of scorn and silence
scenes.rationale: the jeering crowd from behind, Christ silent beyond
scenes.visible_elements: the backs of the jeering crowd, Christ on the cross beyond, silent
scenes.subject_block: the dark backs and raised arms of a jeering crowd in the foreground, beyond them the crucified Christ small and luminous on the cross, head bowed in silence, a pale dusk sky behind the dark hill
scenes.mood_block: atmosphere of loud scorn against quiet majesty

scenes.title: The Hand That Would Not Pull Free
scenes.purpose: the power restrained, in one detail
scenes.rationale: the nailed hand that could have pulled free but stayed
scenes.visible_elements: a close on the nailed open hand against the beam
scenes.subject_block: an extreme close view of a single nailed open hand against the rough grain of the cross beam, the iron nail through the palm, the fingers relaxed and open rather than clenched, a thin dark line of blood, warm raking light on the knuckles, deep shadow beyond
macro_elements: the iron nail in the palm

candidates.title: The Shaking Heads
candidates.rationale: the specific Ps 22:7 gesture, not a generic mob
candidates.visible_elements: jeering faces, shaken heads, shot-out lips at the foot of a cross

candidates.title: David Records the Taunt
candidates.rationale: David writing the mockers' own words
candidates.visible_elements: aged David at a lamp, Hebrew scroll

candidates.title: Twelve Legions Restrained
candidates.visible_elements: Christ on the cross, dim restrained ranks

plan.rationale: Chose the specific Psalm 22:7 gestures (shot-out lip, shaken head, jabbing finger) over a generic mob; two scroll scenes kept distinct (David writing vs the thousand-years diptych); the conviction rendered as sovereign restraint (8) and the Matt 26:53 withheld legions (9); hero is the full cross (11), not the most emotional face (10). Removed the banned token 'frame' from scenes 1 and 6.

panel.note: Scene 1 'The Shaking Heads' opens on the specific Ps 22:7 gesture; arc flows gesture -> scroll -> cross -> love.
Scene 9 'Twelve Legions Restrained' (Matt 26:53) and scene 8 'He Had Every Power' deny impotence visually — matches the locked landing.
Banned token 'frame' removed from scenes 1 and 6; subject_blocks are state-only and Kling-cuttable.
Many passion-Christ scenes — render with the passion ref for face consistency.
Hero #11 (full cross) bookends; #10 face is the emotional climax, correctly not the hero.
Two scroll + several cross scenes — distinct vignettes/framings keep them apart; confirm at render.

gates.evidence: Every beat id in ['hook', 'point', 'proof', 'conviction', 'landing'] has >=1 supporting scene.
shot-out-lip + restrained-legions fresh about the moment, orthodox in claim.
All subject_block + mood_block content clear of the banned-token list.
hero #11 is the cross; lands on Christ.

panel.note: Specific Ps 22:7 gestures anchor the hook; coherent arc to the cross.
Voluntary restraint (Matt 26:53) carried by scenes 8/9/14; denies impotence, matches the landing.
State-only, Kling-cuttable subject_blocks; no banned tokens; macro_elements present per scene.
Render passion-Christ scenes with the passion ref to hold the face.
Cross hero #11 bookends; clean gospel frame.
Watch scroll/cross repetition at render; framings and vignettes differentiate them.

gates.evidence: Every beat id in ['hook', 'point', 'proof', 'conviction', 'landing'] has >=1 supporting scene.
fresh about the moment, orthodox in claim.
All subject_block + mood_block content clear of the banned-token list.

paper_cohesion.notes: The set forms a coherent visual spine on the locked 'deliver' thread: it opens on the specific Psalm 22:7 mockery (shot-out lip, shaken head, jabbing finger), establishes the prophecy with David recording the taunt (scenes 2,3,12), lands the Matthew fulfilment at the cross (scenes 4,5,13), then turns up to the sovereign, restrained Christ (scenes 8,9,14) and closes on willing love (10,11). No theology drift: the conviction scenes deliberately show withheld power (Matt 26:53) and a relaxed, open nailed hand, guarding against any 'He couldn't come down' reading — matching the narration's explicit denial of impotence. Every narration beat is carried by at least one strong scene; hook->climax->close (shaking heads -> sovereign cross -> 'He chose to stay') is a clean spine. Hero #11 is the gospel-pivot cross, so the cut lands on Christ.
